{"credits": [{"type": "analyst", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/165980942?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SepineTam/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SepineTam/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SepineTam/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SepineTam/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/SepineTam", "id": 165980942, "login": "SepineTam", "node_id": "U_kgDOCeSrDg", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SepineTam/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SepineTam/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SepineTam/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SepineTam/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SepineTam/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/SepineTam", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-47708", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 9.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:H/VI:H/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-77", "name": "Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Command ('Command Injection')"}], "description": "### Summary
The `log_file_name` parameter in the `stata_do` API and CLI is directly interpolated into a Stata command string without sanitization. The security guard (`GuardValidator`) only scans the do-file content but does not validate this parameter. An attacker can inject arbitrary Stata commands (including `shell`, `python`, `erase`, etc.) by crafting a malicious `log_file_name` containing quotes, newlines, or Stata command separators.

### Details

In `src/stata_mcp/stata/stata_do/do.py`, both `_execute_unix_like` and `_execute_windows` construct a Stata command string using Python f-strings:

```python
commands = f"""
capture log close
{self.generate_log_command(log_file, is_replace)}
...
do "{dofile_path}"
...
"""
```

The `generate_log_command` method returns:

```python
log_cmd = f'log using "{log_file.as_posix()}", {replace_clause} {log_type} name({log_type}_log)'
```

Where `log_file` is constructed from user-supplied `log_name`:

```python
def generate_log_file(self, log_name: str, extension='log'):
    return self.log_file_path / f"{log_name}.{extension}"
```

The `log_name` parameter comes directly from user input (via MCP tool `stata_do` or CLI `stata-mcp tool do`) without any validation. Since the path is embedded inside double quotes in a Stata command string, an attacker can break out of the string context and inject arbitrary commands.

Additionally, `generate_log_file` does not prevent path traversal via `log_name`, allowing arbitrary file write outside the intended log directory.

### Proof of Concept

When calling `stata_do` via MCP tool with:

```json
{
  "dofile_path": "test.do",
  "log_file_name": "'; shell echo pwned > /tmp/pwned.txt; '"
}
```

The generated Stata commands become:

```stata
log using "<log_dir>/'; shell echo pwned > /tmp/pwned.txt; '.log", replace text name(text_log)
```

Stata interprets this as multiple commands, with `shell echo pwned > /tmp/pwned.txt;` executed as an arbitrary shell command.

### Impact

- **Remote Code Execution** via `shell` command injection
- **Arbitrary file write/overwrite** via path traversal in `log_name`
- Complete bypass of the security guard, as the guard only validates do-file content, not wrapper parameters

### Remediation / Fix

1. Apply strict allowlist validation to `log_name` (only alphanumeric, underscore, dot, hyphen; max 128 chars)
2. Resolve and verify the constructed log path remains within the intended log directory
3. Consider generating safe internal filenames (e.g., UUIDs) instead of accepting user-defined log names for command construction
4. Apply similar sanitization to `dofile_path` before embedding it into Stata command strings

### References

- Issue: #74
- Fix commit: https://github.com/SepineTam/stata-mcp/commit/e6f945941ae0c7cf5e74a428e0b3dc82b396382f", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00629, "percentile": 0.70833}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-4p62-hqp5-g644", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-04T21:00:42Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4p62-hqp5-g644", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-4p62-hqp5-g644"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-47708"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-04T21:00:42Z", "references": ["https://github.com/SepineTam/stata-mcp/security/advisories/GHSA-4p62-hqp5-g644", "https://github.com/SepineTam/mcp-for-stata/issues/74", "https://github.com/SepineTam/mcp-for-stata/commit/e6f945941ae0c7cf5e74a428e0b3dc82b396382f", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4p62-hqp5-g644"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/SepineTam/mcp-for-stata/security-advisories/GHSA-4p62-hqp5-g644", "severity": "critical", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/SepineTam/stata-mcp", "summary": "MCP-for-Stata: Command injection via log_file_name parameter in Stata command wrapper", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-04T21:00:42Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-4p62-hqp5-g644", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.17.3", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "stata-mcp"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 1.17.3"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: MCP-for-Stata: Command injection via log_file_name parameter in Stata command wrapper
